title: Continue Basics live Multiplication timeout investigation after spawn fixes
description:
Goal
Continue the Basics live Multiplication timeout investigation across NBNv2-demos and the sibling NBNv2 runtime repo. The next agent may start and stop processes from either repo, add instrumentation, and make careful code changes in either repo as needed. Ask the user to run something only if necessary.

Current state as of April 2, 2026
- The user explicitly wants a fresh agent instance because prior context was long and dirty.
- Runtime and Basics UI were closed after the latest run; the next agent may start its own processes as needed.
- Both repos were clean and pushed at the end of the prior session.
- NBNv2-demos main was at commit 155e230 when the last code change landed.
- NBNv2 main was at commit 82b36f7 when the last runtime change landed.

Current blocking symptom
- The old immediate generation-1 spawn-collapse has improved substantially, but the full Multiplication live run is still not healthy.
- The most recent 4-worker run progressed far past initial spawn/placement, but generation 1 still failed.
- The current failure is no longer the old early tick-timeout storm. The current failure is in per-sample evaluation/reset.

Most recent live run to inspect first
- Basics UI run log:
  - /home/hailey/AI/NBNv2-demos/Basics/artifacts/ui-runs/basics-ui-multiplication-20260402-003019.jsonl
- Runtime logs:
  - /home/hailey/.local/share/Nbn.Workbench/logs/HiveMind.out.log
  - /home/hailey/.local/share/Nbn.Workbench/logs/IoGateway.out.log

What the latest 4-worker run actually did
- Run started at 2026-04-02T00:30:19Z.
- EligibleWorkerCount=4, InitialPopulationCount=24, Setup concurrency=4.
- The run got through:
  - spawn requests
  - placement visibility
  - runtime config
  - output sink registration
  - at least some brains entering evaluating samples
- It did not reproduce the older symptom where generation 1 failed almost immediately with spawn_request_failed plus TickDeliver timeout storm.
- Later in the run, sample progress still never advanced past 0/600.
- The terminal snapshots were:
  - Batch 1/1 finished in about 1081.7s
  - average queue wait about 584.3s per brain
  - average spawn request about 128.2s per brain
  - average setup about 42.1s per brain
  - average observation 0.00s per brain
  - failures: evaluation_failed x24
- Final execution failure detail in the Basics log:
  - Generation 1 produced no viable evaluations.
  - Representative failures were reset_brain_runtime_state failures, including:
    - hivemind_request_failed: Request didn't receive any Response within the expected time
    - runtime_reset_failed: Request didn't receive any Response within the expected time
- This strongly suggests the current bottleneck moved from initial spawn/placement to runtime reset between samples.

What improved already
1. IO gateway disappearance / stale endpoint handling
- Earlier problem: Basics often failed with io-gateway no longer exists.
- This was investigated across Basics reconnect logic and runtime IO gateway behavior.
- Current understanding: that specific symptom is no longer the main blocker in the latest run.

2. Per-brain coordinator disappearance under load
- NBNv2-h7c was completed by another agent.
- The runtime now suppresses idle dirty-on-change input drains.
- That did not fully solve the live Basics problem, but it changed the failure shape.

3. Pause/resume through IO
- NBNv2 commit 88d5eb6: Forward pause and resume through IO gateway.
- Basics initially used a follow-up PauseBrain command after spawn.
- This helped but still left a race: some brains entered the first tick window before the pause landed.

4. Atomic paused spawn
- NBNv2 commit 82b36f7: Add atomic paused spawn support.
- Added nbn.control.SpawnBrain.start_paused.
- Basics commit 5228b8b: Use paused spawn for Basics evaluation.
- This removed the old immediate generation-1 collapse pattern.

5. Longer Basics spawn/request budgets
- NBNv2-demos commit 155e230: Increase Basics spawn timeout defaults.
- Basics RequestTimeout default and UI default were raised from 30s to 120s.
- This prevented the prior client-side spawn_request_failed that occurred simply because queued placement exceeded 30 seconds.

Relevant commit history
NBNv2
- 88d5eb6 Forward pause and resume through IO gateway
- 9956240 Suppress idle dirty-on-change input drains
- 82b36f7 Add atomic paused spawn support

NBNv2-demos
- ce59acd Pause Basics brains through setup
- 5228b8b Use paused spawn for Basics evaluation
- 155e230 Increase Basics spawn timeout defaults

What did not work or is no longer the main issue
- Simply adding per-worker spawn cadence delays was not sufficient.
- Simply reusing the old PauseBrain-after-spawn approach was not sufficient; it lost a race.
- The current problem should not be treated as the same bug as the old immediate spawn collapse unless the newest logs show that symptom again.

Current best diagnosis
- The latest run shows that spawn and placement are now getting much further than before.
- The remaining blocker is likely in the ResetBrainRuntimeState path used before each sample in Basics.
- In the last run, all 24 evaluations failed before any sample progress was recorded.
- The failure text points to timeouts inside reset_brain_runtime_state, sometimes coming back as hivemind_request_failed and sometimes runtime_reset_failed.
- Investigate the barrier/reset path under concurrent live load, not just spawn.

Suggested next steps for the next agent
1. Reproduce against the latest commits, not older binaries.
- Ensure runtime processes are actually running from NBNv2 commit 82b36f7 or later.
- Ensure Basics UI is running from NBNv2-demos commit 155e230 or later.

2. Start with the latest logs, not older hypotheses.
- Read the end of basics-ui-multiplication-20260402-003019.jsonl.
- Confirm the failure is still reset_brain_runtime_state under current binaries.

3. Inspect/reset instrumentation around runtime reset.
- Basics path to inspect:
  - Basics/src/Basics.Environment/BasicsExecutionSession.cs
  - ObserveSampleAsync and the reset_brain_runtime_state call
- Runtime path to inspect:
  - Nbn.Runtime.IO coordinator routing for ResetBrainRuntimeState
  - HiveMind barrier/reset handling
  - BrainSignalRouter reset behavior
  - RegionShard reset handling
- If needed, add timing logs around:
  - reset request issued by Basics
  - reset forwarded by IO
  - reset queued/applied at HiveMind barrier
  - reset ack returned from shards/router

4. Determine whether the right fix is timeout, concurrency, or sequencing.
- Possibilities include:
  - reset budget too low for concurrent live runs
  - too many concurrent resets in flight
  - reset path serialized behind work in a way Basics is not accounting for
  - one or more brains getting stuck waiting for a barrier condition that never clears under current setup
- Do not assume increasing timeouts is the right final fix unless instrumentation shows real but slow progress.

5. Validate live after changes.
- The real acceptance bar is not just tests.
- Confirm a live Multiplication run can get through generation 1 without unrecoverable failure and with sample progress moving beyond 0/600.

Verification history already done

Operator permissions / constraints

  - launch and stop processes from either repo as needed
  - change code in either repo carefully as needed

comments.text: Partial mitigation landed in Basics: EventedOutput no longer silently falls back to the ready lane in the vector stream when no ready OutputEvent arrives. comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Additional Basics-side mitigation landed: sample input delivery is now an acknowledged IO request instead of fire-and-forget, so resume no longer races ahead of input arrival. comments.author: hailey
comments.text: The stricter EventedOutput vector-threshold check turned out to be too strong for current multiplication brains: after rebuild/restart on 2026-04-08 21:24 MDT, generation 1 failed 32/32 with ready_window_exhausted even though ready_events_seen was typically 3 and last_vector_tick matched last_ready_tick. comments.author: hailey

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: New findings from 2026-04-02 live investigation: the long UI outliers are not UI-vs-CLI noise; they cluster around placement visibility waits. A safe NBN runtime fix likely needs IO Gateway AwaitSpawnPlacementViaIO to avoid serializing long waits on one actor mailbox, but the first reentrant attempt exposed a separate direct-IO timeout/cleanup dependency in Nbn.Tests.HiveMind.HiveMindSpawnBrainTests.SpawnBrainViaIo_AfterFailedSpawnTimeout_CanStillPlaceANewBrain. Landed demos-side mitigation instead: Basics now uses a 5s default placement wait, a 250ms retry backoff after retryable spawn failures, and accuracy-first best-so-far tracking with fitness as the tie-breaker. Worker-close hardening also landed via LocalWorkerProcessService process-exit fallback cleanup. Runtime-side safe reentrancy remains open here.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Resolved in ../NBNv2: IO Gateway no longer blocks its actor mailbox while awaiting AwaitSpawnPlacementViaIO. The fix moves the long HiveMind wait plus metadata-visibility probe off the live mailbox onto root-context async work, then replies directly to the original requester. Also added a regression proving concurrent AwaitSpawnPlacementViaIO calls overlap instead of serializing through the gateway mailbox, and adjusted the failed-timeout-then-retry test to match real client cleanup before retrying on the same constrained worker lane. Verification: targeted IO/HiveMind spawn-wait slice, docs freshness check, and full dotnet test NBNv2.sln (1152 passed). title: Basics: continue XOR convergence tuning past 0.5 plateau
description: After the task-aware XOR UI default rollout, the current single-worker evented profile reaches generation 5 within a 240s live trial but still plateaus at accuracy≈0.5 and fitness≈0.528. Continue profile/runtime tuning until XOR shows upward generation-over-generation progress on the live stack.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Fresh live-stack defaults sweep on 2026-03-30 after the vector-segment demo fix and NBNv2-4mp spawn/placement work. Explicit worker-count/frequency experiments for XOR showed: 1 worker with vector-potential rich profile (pop=4, runs=4, concurrent=2) reached generation 17 in 120s and repeatedly hit accuracy=0.75 fitness=0.764 while oscillating; 2 workers with the same rich profile and concurrent=2 reached generation 3 in 120s with accuracy history 0.5,0.75,0.75 and best fitness 0.764; 2 workers with concurrent=4 went deeper faster but failed at generation 7 with output_timeout_or_width_mismatch x4; 4-worker runs at 8x4 and 4x4 both regressed into output_timeout_or_width_mismatch failures. Current practical lesson: moderate pressure (roughly one active spawn per worker, or the same 4x2 profile on one worker) is better than aggressive batching for XOR on the live stack. Additional per-task probes for UI defaults: OR conservative evented exact-template 1/1/1 solved immediately in generation 1 with accuracy=1 fitness=1; AND conservative evented exact-template repeatedly reached accuracy=0.75 fitness=0.764 early but oscillated instead of stopping; GT conservative evented exact-template repeatedly reached accuracy=0.667 fitness=0.685; Multiplication with a richer vector-potential profile (same seed/scheduling as the rich XOR profile, pop=4, runs=4, concurrent=2) reached roughly accuracy=0.32 fitness=0.583 by generation 5 before timeout. I am rolling these into BasicsTaskExecutionProfiles so the UI auto-applies task-specific defaults while preserving operator override.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Multiplication-specific follow-up on 2026-03-30 after the broader defaults sweep: the richer multiplication profile using vector-potential or vector-buffer at pop=4, runs=4, concurrent=2 still reproduced the UI-style generation-2 failure mode or hung entering generation 2 under live load. A safer multiplication profile using continuous-buffer with the same richer seed shape but reduced pressure (pop=2, runs=2, concurrent=1, maxRunsPerPair=4) survived through generation 4 in a 75s live trial without reproducing the earlier batch-2 all-timeout collapse. Best observed metrics in that safer run were roughly accuracy=0.28 and fitness=0.494 by generation 2 before later oscillation/timeout. Rolling Multiplication defaults to that safer buffer profile is the pragmatic fix for current UI use.

title: Basics: add long-horizon multiplication evolution regression

title: Basics: bootstrap first external-world demo
description: Track the first real `Basics` demo in this repo. Scope includes the shared environment/orchestration contract, the small operator UI, and one task-plugin issue per initial `2 -> 1` Basics task.
acceptance_criteria:
- The `Basics` environment, UI, and task-plugin work is represented by child issues in a usable implementation order.
- The issue tree keeps `Basics` aligned with supported External World contracts rather than ad hoc runtime internals.
- Resource-sizing and runtime-gap decisions are explicit before coding starts.

title: Basics: create environment and orchestration contract
description: Create the shared `Basics` environment/orchestration contract that every first-wave task plugin will use. This includes the random initial population shape, reproduction/speciation settings, deterministic scoring interface, metrics contract, and the capacity-aware defaults for initial brain count, reproduction run count, and maximum concurrent brains.
design: Use supported IO/repro/speciation surfaces (Connect, GetPlacementWorkerInventory, SpawnBrainViaIO, BrainInfoRequest, output subscriptions, reproduction wrappers, speciation wrappers). The shared environment is template-seeded rather than unconstrained-random, and IO now provides placement-filtered worker capacity for sizing.
acceptance_criteria:
- Define the `Basics` environment scaffold and standardized task-plugin interface under `Basics` for brains with exactly `input_width=2` and `output_width=1`.
- Validate spawned brains through `BrainInfo` before scoring and reject any brain that does not present `2/1` geometry.
- Random-brain constraints and default reproduction settings preserve IO invariants: input region `0`, output region `31`, no axon targets to region `0`, no output-region self-targeting, and default `protect_io_region_neuron_counts` behavior.
- Document or implement the system-capacity sizing strategy for population size, reproduction `run_count`, and max concurrent brains, including manual overrides and deterministic fallback behavior.
- Define the shared metrics contract consumed by the UI: accuracy/fitness over time, best individual, aggregate population stats, and reproduction throughput/capacity summaries.
notes: Implemented Basics solution/library/tests/README plus the runtime IO wrapper, docs, and regression tests for IO-only worker-capacity queries.
close_reason: Implemented the shared Basics environment library, tests, README, template-seeded population contract, parent-selection/run-allocation policy, and the IO-only placement-worker capacity query with runtime docs/tests.

title: Basics task plugin: One-hot classifier
description: Investigate and, if viable, implement a `One-hot classifier` task plugin for the `Basics` environment. The current `Basics` scope is still `2 -> 1`, so this issue should first resolve whether a one-hot-style framing fits that contract or should be reframed/deferred.
acceptance_criteria:

- If it is viable, define and document the canonical dataset, encoding, output interpretation, and deterministic scoring contract.
- If it is not viable, document the mismatch clearly and propose a replacement or reframing that still fits the `Basics` curriculum.
- Emit or specify the standardized score breakdown expected by the shared environment and UI.

title: Basics: execution loop and Start/Stop controls

Scope includes spawning the template-seeded initial population, evaluating selected task plugins against running brains, selecting parents, allocating run counts within capacity bounds, reproduction/speciation orchestration, and streaming live metrics back into the UI.

- Add Start/Stop controls that drive a real execution loop rather than configuration-only preflight.
- Spawn and manage the template-anchored initial population within the current seed-shape and divergence bounds.
- Evaluate the selected Basics task plugin against running brains and feed standardized metric samples back to the UI.
- Select parents and allocate reproduction run counts using the shared scheduling policy while staying within capacity-derived bounds.
- Update live UI metrics/charts during execution and surface clear runtime failure states.
notes: Starting an AND-scoped first pass. Current concrete gap is seed-template publication/generation; execution loop work will be built around the implemented AND plugin and explicit template artifact handling.
close_reason: Implemented the AND-first execution loop with Start/Stop, template publication, live evaluation, reproduction/speciation orchestration, and UI metrics updates.

The UI now offers OutputEvent, continuous potential, and continuous buffer; the execution loop defaults to continuous potential for deterministic scoring, supports sparse OutputEvent evaluation as an explicit option, and switches vector source through a new IO-level runtime setter. Residual limitation is tracked separately in demos-x3j.13 because the vector source setter is runtime-global today, not per-brain.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Addressed three user-facing problems: seed-shape boxes now show concrete default/current placeholders instead of 'template'; speciation startup now verifies that the epoch actually advanced and no longer hides failed assignment decisions behind local species fallbacks; and execution snapshots now surface epoch/failure diagnostics while sample observation retries missing outputs before failing a member. Remaining live-runtime automation work is tracked in demos-x3j.14.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Hardened the Basics execution/runtime loop around live trials and local smoke: execution now preserves the last good metrics on cancel/failure instead of resetting to zero, full-generation evaluation failures stop the run immediately instead of spinning through zero-fitness generations, the runtime client consumes IO BrainTerminated events for teardown awareness, and smoke-local can now honor full harness configs while the in-process local runtime no longer ages out its only worker after ~10s due the stale worker-inventory window. Residual convergence work is tracked separately in demos-n4a.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Closed demos-n4a after wiring variation-band controls into reproduction, reclassifying timeout-driven stopped runs correctly in the live harness, routing Connect through the permanent runtime receiver for lifecycle broadcasts, and relaxing the local in-process placement/inventory timeouts so local-live trials can actually progress across generations.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Follow-up polish after close: the real UI run was still failing in generation 1 because potential-mode brains were redundantly issuing per-brain output-vector-source writes and swallowing request failures into 'spawn_failed:unknown'. The execution path now skips the setter when BrainInfo already reports the requested vector source, runtime-client request failures return explicit synthetic failure codes instead of null/unknown, and the UI no longer pre-populates aggressive active sizing overrides (256/8/128) so capacity-driven settings remain the default until the operator explicitly overrides them.

title: Basics: per-run output source isolation
description: Track the remaining limitation after output-mode selection landed: vector source switching currently changes the runtime-wide output-vector policy through IO, which is good enough for a single demo session but not isolated per run/brain. Follow-up scope is per-run or per-brain isolation, clearer UI surfacing of the current runtime-wide effect, and any runtime contract changes needed to remove the shared global side effect.
close_reason: Per-brain output vector source isolation is implemented, documented, and verified across NBNv2 and Basics.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Implemented per-brain output vector source isolation for Basics. IO and control SetOutputVectorSource now accept optional brain_id, HiveMind tracks per-brain output vector source plus explicit overrides, global changes now only retarget brains still following the runtime default, and Basics applies vector mode per spawned evaluation brain. Docs and regression coverage were updated, including a HiveMind test proving a brain-scoped override survives a later global default change while another brain follows the new default.

title: Basics: automated live trial harness
description: Track a deeper live-runtime feedback loop for Basics: repeatable automated trial runs against a running NBN stack, richer per-generation instrumentation, and a path for agent-driven tuning until the demo achieves stable and decent real runtime results instead of only fake-runtime confidence.
close_reason: Live trial harness, structured telemetry, and basic auto-tuning are implemented for Basics and verified in the demo repo.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Implemented a headless Basics live harness in src/Basics.Harness plus reusable live-trial orchestration in Basics.Environment. The harness now repeats real IO-backed trials against a running stack, emits structured per-generation snapshot telemetry into JSON reports, and can apply simple follow-up tuning between trials. Added focused harness tests and README coverage, plus ignored artifact output under Basics/artifacts/.

title: Basics: validate live IO-connected runs and retune defaults
description: Track the remaining work after the harness-side connect retry fixes: rerun Basics against a fresh real IO-connected stack, confirm reliable generation-1 evaluation, gather at least generation-5 progress evidence, and update operator defaults/bounds from that evidence.
close_reason: Completed task-aware UI defaults rollout and live validation; remaining vector-mode regression and deeper XOR convergence work split to demos-04b and demos-bie.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Current status before the next live-stack pass: the original generation-1 failure path no longer reproduces in the same way; real live runs now reach nonzero generation-1 evaluations, and the harness has been hardened so connect-window expiry returns ConnectFailed instead of crashing. Remaining work depends on a fresh live IO stack to validate repeated connects, generation progression through >=5, and any resulting default changes.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Real AND live-stack findings after the spawn/setup fixes: conservative profiles now connect and execute reliably on the live IO stack. Verified runs include potential-mode 8/2/1 and exact-template 1/1/1, plus buffer-mode exact-template 1/1/1; the exact-template profiles show generation-over-generation improvement from gen1 accuracy=0.75 fitness=0.764 to gen2 accuracy=1 fitness=1. Additional demo-side fixes landed for setup spawn throttling, longer teardown waits, richer spawn failure diagnostics, and preserving terminal success snapshots when a long-running trial hits its wall-clock timeout during cleanup. Moderate 16/2/4 potential-mode runs still consume the full 240s budget and are therefore a defaults/efficiency question rather than a basic correctness blocker for AND.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Explicit WorkerNode experiments on the live stack: starting WorkerNodes directly from the CLI works and the runtime can discover multiple workers on one machine when they use distinct advertised ports/actor names. A two-worker XOR probe did increase plan inventory to EligibleWorkerCount=2, but under the tested 4/1/2 profile it still failed in generation 1 with spawn request timeouts and missing BrainInfo responses instead of showing a clear throughput win. Current conclusion: worker-launch support in Basics may still be useful later, but it is not yet justified as an immediate UI responsibility based on the observed live-task results.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Task-specific defaults should auto-apply once we have evidence for them. For Basics UI, the desired behavior is: when the selected task changes, apply the current sane default seed-shape/reproduction/sizing profile for that task automatically (until the operator explicitly overrides). XOR is the current forcing function for this work because the live 1-worker exact-template profile reaches 5+ generations but plateaus around 0.5 accuracy / 0.528 fitness instead of steadily improving toward 100%.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Runtime-side placement follow-up landed in ../NBNv2: for same-locality worker candidates, HiveMind now breaks ties on hosted-brain count before transient process CPU load when choosing workers for new placements. This is a narrow balancing tweak aimed at same-host multi-worker brain distribution; planner + orchestration suites and the full NBN runtime suite passed after the change.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Harder-task live findings after the same-host HiveMind placement tweak: explicit 4-worker startup works when WorkerNodes are launched with distinct advertised ports/actor names and a 95% storage limit. HiveMind then reports EligibleWorkerCount=4. However, XOR still does not benefit automatically from more workers: a 4-worker 8/2/4 profile failed in generation 1 with spawn request timeouts and missing BrainInfo, while a lower-pressure 4-worker 4/2/1 profile evaluated cleanly but regressed from gen1 accuracy=0.5 fitness=0.528 to gen2 accuracy=0.25 fitness=0.292 before timing out. One-worker XOR remains the cleanest diagnostic baseline because it reaches 5+ generations without setup collapse, even though it plateaus/oscillates instead of improving steadily. Current conclusion: multi-worker launch is not yet justified as a Basics UI responsibility; the next pressure point is XOR-specific defaults/template/reproduction tuning rather than raw worker count.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Latest XOR tuning pass: keeping to one worker is still the right baseline. Multi-worker capacity and the same-host placement tweak are real, but worker count alone did not solve XOR. Stable 1-worker XOR still reaches 5+ generations but plateaus/oscillates around 0.5 accuracy / 0.528 fitness. A richer task-aware seed profile (1 region, 2-3 internal neurons, 6-10 axons, function mutation enabled, higher diversity/exploration) did not immediately solve the plateau; instead, exact-template runs often failed in generation 1 with output_timeout_or_width_mismatch. That points away from pure resource scarcity and toward a task/template/observation-path issue that should be fixed before more parameter sweeps. Desired next step: introduce explicit task-specific default profiles and add a focused XOR execution regression before more live tuning.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Demo-side observation-path fix landed during XOR tuning: vector-mode evaluation no longer primes with a hard-coded 0,0 input. It now chooses a prime vector from the task dataset that differs from the first sample's expected output when possible, and vector retries can send a separator input before resubmitting the stalled sample. A focused XOR session regression now covers change-sensitive vector emission. This does not solve XOR by itself, but it removes one class of false plateaus/timeouts from the tuning loop.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Completed the task-aware UI default rollout based on fresh live-stack evidence. New Basics task profiles now auto-apply when the selected UI task changes while still allowing manual override. XOR currently auto-selects an evented-output single-worker profile (1 internal region, 2-3 internal neurons, 6-10 axons, function mutation enabled, pop=2, runs=4, concurrent=1, higher diversity/exploration). BasicsExecutionSession now uses a 1s observation timeout for evented output mode so sparse/non-firing outputs no longer burn 10s per sample. Full Basics test suite passed after the change. Live stack verification on 2026-03-29: vector-potential and vector-buffer still fail immediately with output_timeout_or_width_mismatch on both AND and XOR, but the new XOR evented default reaches generation 5 inside a 240s live trial on one worker without setup collapse (accuracy history: 0.5,0.5,0.5,0.5,0.5; fitness history: 0.5278 repeated). Follow-ups filed: demos-04b for vector-mode compatibility and demos-bie for convergence tuning beyond the 0.5 plateau.

title: Basics: create configuration and metrics UI
description: Create the small `Basics` UI for connection/configuration, capacity-derived defaults, runtime overrides, and metrics/plotting. This should follow the environment contract rather than inventing its own task or population model.
design: Use IO GetPlacementWorkerInventory for capacity discovery. SettingsMonitor fields become optional/operator-only for workflows outside runtime sizing, rather than mandatory for the Basics environment.
acceptance_criteria:
- The UI captures the IO endpoint, with additional service endpoints optional for operator workflows rather than required for capacity discovery.
- After connection, the operator can fetch current system capacity through IO and apply suggested defaults for initial population size, reproduction `run_count`, and max concurrent brains.
- The operator can override auto-sized settings and edit the core environment/task parameters without bypassing validation.
- The UI exposes task selection, relevant reproduction/speciation settings, and clear connection/runtime error states.
- The UI plots or otherwise surfaces accuracy/fitness over time plus best-individual and population-level metrics defined by the shared environment contract.
notes: Started implementing the Basics UI project on top of the shared environment contract and the new IO capacity query.
close_reason: Implemented the Basics UI shell and configuration workflow on top of the shared environment contract, including IO-only capacity discovery and live-ready metric/chart surfaces.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Implemented a new Avalonia Basics UI project with IO-only capacity fetch, task selection, template-family configuration, bounded divergence controls, reproduction/speciation scheduling controls, override application, validation/error surfacing, and chart/metric panels that are ready for live samples once task plugins begin publishing data.

comments.author: hailey

title: Basics task plugin: AND

- Emit standardized score breakdown data consumable by the shared environment and UI.

title: Investigate deterministic region-31 shard load failures after placement-header fallback
description: Live Basics UI run basics-ui-multiplication-20260403-045040 still produced spawn_failed:spawn_internal_error for a region 31 shard after the transient artifact-load retry fix. The strongest current hypothesis is HiveMind placement planning silently falling back to IO-width-only regions when BaseDefinition exists but TryReadPlacementHeader fails, which can synthesize a bad output-region span and later surface as a WorkerNode region-31 loader failure. Follow up by failing closed or otherwise tightening placement-header fallback, add a deterministic regression around the output-region mismatch path, and keep the fuller worker/UI diagnostics landed in this session.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: Additional live evidence from basics-ui-multiplication-20260403-051858: after rebuild/restart, the initial WorkerNode artifact-load race surfaced as explicit concurrent collection corruption during artifact-backed shard loads on worker-02 (region 0 and region 1), and later generations mostly showed ready_timeout with ~12-16s batch durations. The collection-race and oversized per-assignment timeout arming are fixed in ../NBNv2 in this session; demos still needs follow-up on the deterministic placement-header fallback / region-31 path if it continues after the next rebuild.

title: Validate post-fix live Basics placement behavior and tune if needed
description: After landing the runtime AwaitSpawnPlacement total-budget fix plus concurrent artifact-cache staging fix, run fresh Basics UI validation on Multiplication/EventedOutput and confirm the remaining slow-batch pattern is materially reduced. If live stalls remain, use the now-correct placement-wait timing to decide whether another runtime placement/readiness budget or pacing change is warranted.

comments.author: hailey
comments.text: This session materially tightened the remaining placement wait path before any new live rerun: AwaitSpawnPlacement default waits no longer extend across retry-only churn, accepted-but-not-ready assignments fail within the single-attempt timeout window, and worker-side deferred artifact-backed shard preparation now has bounded backlog with retryable failure instead of long accepted stalls. Basics also lands best-brain ready-tick/sample-repeat UI plumbing. Live rebuild validation still required, so this stays open.
